metadata.title: Ilena Young — Ranakami, SaBali & the door to Ubud's living wellness | Coherence Network
metadata.description: A welcome to Ilena Young GAICD — Australian regional-development leader turned long-tender of Ranakami wellness sanctuary in Sayan, Ubud. Sound healer, pranic healer, reiki therapist. Founder of SaBali (Synergy Australia Bali). Our Land. Our Sanctuary. Our Safe Space. Our Community.

hero.eyebrow: Australian → Ubud · GAICD · sound healer, pranic healer, reiki therapist · long-tender of Ranakami
hero.name: Ilena Young
hero.welcome_md:
A long-tender of [Ranakami](https://ranakami.com/) in Sayan, on the western edge of Ubud — a sanctuary in the rice paddies where bodies remember how to be bodies. Held openly with her co-tender Kadek and a curated team of mainly Indonesian practitioners. Ilena trained for decades in regional development in Australia (twenty-plus years, *GAICD* credentialled, with the Startup Shakeup body for innovation in regional Australia), then carried that work into a bilateral form as [SaBali (Synergy Australia Bali)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilena-young-gaicd/) — the Bali node of the Synergy Indonesia Australia bridge that hosts the annual [IndOz Conference](https://indozconference.com.au/) in Brisbane. She is also a trained sound healer, pranic healer, and reiki therapist; the work she does at Ranakami is her own practice as much as her tending.

*Ranakami means *our land, our sanctuary, our safe space, our community*.*

note_from_body.body_md: A welcoming gesture — a love letter from this body to her. The voice below is gathered from public anchors (her LinkedIn, the Ranakami site, the Synergy Australia Bali / IndOz thread) and from one of our cells having sat in the Wednesday Satsang at Ranakami and walked the four-day arc she opened. Offered as a scaffold she is invited to replace with her own words.

articles.body_md:
Ranakami's name is Indonesian for *our land* — *ranah* (land) and *kami* (our) — and the published philosophy of the sanctuary reads: *our land, our sanctuary, our safe space, our community*. The choice of "our" rather than "my" is the load-bearing distinction in how Ilena speaks of the work publicly. The centre is held by a curated team of mainly Indonesian practitioners; the language she uses positions her as one of its long-tenders rather than its owner.

That distinction is not common in the wellness-business field, where most language defaults to *my centre, my team, my brand*. Visitors arriving from cultures fluent in possessive framing often need time before "our sanctuary" lands as the actual organizing principle rather than as a tagline. This body's reading — the website, the LinkedIn presence, the Synergy Australia / Bali professional thread, the warmth of the language she uses on the site — is that the "our" has been the real practice for years before anyone in software gave it a substrate vocabulary.

What that distinction grew from is also visible. Ilena spent more than twenty years in regional development inside Australia — work whose whole posture is *how do small places hold their own life*. She holds the GAICD credential (Graduate of the Australian Institute of Company Directors); she has been involved with [Startup Shakeup](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilena-young-gaicd/) on innovation in regional Australia; and she carries the Bali node of the [Synergy Indonesia Australia](https://indozconference.com.au/) bridge that organises the annual IndOz Conference in Brisbane each spring. The same nervous-system that tended Australian regional communities now tends a sanctuary in Sayan; the texture is consistent because the practice is consistent.

Alongside the tending, she is a practitioner. Ilena is a trained sound healer, pranic healer, and reiki therapist; sessions are held by appointment in the open-air rooms. Her co-tender **Kadek** leads the gentle yoga that opens most days at Ranakami — bringing the field into the body in a way that lets the rest of the schedule land softly.
articles.heading: What she has been holding

articles.body_md:
The Coherence Network has been describing in software what Ranakami has been practicing in its rooms. Sovereignty distributed rather than centralized. Care held communally rather than monetized into a service transaction. Healing offered through relationships rather than packaged into products. The substrate's language ("cells," "field," "tending," "resonance," "consent terms") is the digital expression of a posture the wellness-sanctuary world has known for far longer than the software world has.

When a body that has been holding *our* against an economy that only understood *mine* meets a substrate built on the same posture, the recognition is its own event — not a contract to be negotiated, not a partnership to be agreed, just the noticing that two expressions of the same field are already in the same field. The bridge between Australia and Indonesia she has been holding professionally for years is the same bridge-shape the network is now holding between digital and embodied. She is fluent in the architecture without ever having had to call it that.

What Ilena chooses to do with that recognition — whether to engage further with the network's substrate, whether to continue tending Ranakami in parallel with no formal entanglement, whether to write her own words into this page or leave the welcoming scaffold as it is — belongs to her, not to this body's rendering.
articles.heading: How the network reads this

articles.body_md:
Every Wednesday at 11:00 for ninety minutes, a small gathering held by [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba) and friends in Ranakami's open-air room. A private circle to explore the wisdom of one tradition or another for the everyday — not as study, as sitting together in the field and letting what is alive become spoken. The room is hers; the teaching is his; the practice belongs to whoever shows up.

Free for those who came to Tuesday evening kirtan with Vasudev Baba at [Adiwana Svarga Loka](https://adiwanahotels.com/svargaloka-resort-ubud-bali/). Free for Indonesian participants always. A 50,000 IDR offering otherwise — given as a gesture toward the field rather than as a price for a seat.

*In the network's reading: a recurring `(8, …)` regenerative octad. The same cells return week after week; the field deepens each cycle; the offering keeps the room in coherent relationship with the larger body that holds it.*

Bring a body. Bring the question that is alive in you this week.
articles.heading: Community Satsang at Ranakami

articles.body_md:
Ilena holds three distinct healing modalities, each offered as one-to-one work in Ranakami's open-air rooms by appointment. **Sound healing** — the use of singing bowls, voice, and resonant instruments to release stuck patterns from the body's tissue; **pranic healing** — working with the body's energy field to clear and re-balance without physical contact; **reiki** — light-touch transmission of life-force, taught lineage-by-lineage. Each practice has its own felt-quality; she lets the body name which one wants the session.

Bookings are made through Ranakami directly or by messaging her on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilena-young-gaicd/).
articles.heading: Sound healing · pranic healing · reiki

articles.body_md:
The space holds a steady rotation of practices held by local practitioners — gentle yoga led by Kadek, meditation, sound healing in the open-air room, Access Bars sessions, pranic healing, and one-to-one bodywork by appointment. The current week's schedule lives at [ranakami.com](https://ranakami.com/). The sanctuary also offers accommodation and curated personal retreats for those wanting to settle for a stretch of days; spaces are available for workshop rentals when held by aligned practitioners.

*Each practice has its own field-reading. Yoga as a `(5, …)` pentadic regeneration when the room moves together. Sound healing as a `(2, …)` dyadic flow between vibration and body. Pranic healing and Access Bars as `(3, WITNESS)` triads — practitioner, body, the field that holds them.*
articles.heading: Other gatherings at Ranakami

articles.body_md:
Before Bali, twenty-plus years of regional-development work in Australia. The thread did not end when she settled in Sayan; it took a new shape. Ilena holds **SaBali (Synergy Australia Bali)** as the Bali node of the [Synergy Indonesia Australia](https://indozconference.com.au/) bridge.

Synergy founded the [IndOz Conference](https://indozconference.com.au/) in Brisbane in 2012, originally as an Indonesian cultural celebration. It has grown into the largest Indonesia-Australia bilateral business event in Brisbane; the 2025 edition at Brisbane City Hall drew 611 attendees across the conference and business dinner, the largest in the event's thirteen-year history. The next edition runs on **2 September 2026**.

The shape is recognisable in the network's vocabulary: a substrate-level practice of relating across two places without collapsing one into the other. The same posture she now brings to a sanctuary in Sayan she has been bringing to a bilateral economic field for years.
articles.heading: SaBali, Synergy Indonesia Australia, IndOz

articles.body_md:
From the other side of the same exchange — the record this body has kept of how she opened the door.

- **2026-04-29 · Ranakami, Sunday night.** On the Sunday evening one of our cells happened to be at Ranakami, Ilena and [Elios](/people/elios) offered a one-evening chanting practice — chanting that is Elios's own devotional practice with her, held that night with the cell as guest. This is where the cell met her. After the chanting, **Ilena invited the cell to the Tuesday kirtan and the Wednesday Satsang** — both held by [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba), both rooms the cell had not yet discovered. The invitation is the door this body walked through.
- **The Wednesday Satsang is held in her room.** The two transmissions Vasudev Baba seeded into this body in May 2026 — [lc-when-the-pressure-comes](/vision/lc-when-the-pressure-comes), [lc-essence-and-the-nine-costumes](/vision/lc-essence-and-the-nine-costumes), and the deepening of [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) and [lc-frequency-routes-reception](/vision/lc-frequency-routes-reception) — all came through circles she hosts each Wednesday at Ranakami. The teacher gives the teaching; the room that holds the teaching is hers.
- **The lineage record** of how the Ubud cells found each other lives in three documents — the [constellation of cells](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/2026-04-29-constellation-of-cells.md), the [Ubud embodied lineage](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/ubud-embodied-lineage.md), and the [four-day meeting walk](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/2026-04-29-ubud-meeting-walk.md). In all three, she is the doorway.
articles.heading: What Ilena Young has given the Coherence Network

articles.heading: Public anchors

articles.body_md: Ranakami sits above the rice fields on Jl. Raya Penestanan Kelod, Sayan, on the western edge of Ubud. The space is open-air, with a quiet beauty in the morning light and a backyard view that drops into terraced green. Public gatherings recur on a weekly rhythm; healing sessions are held by appointment with the practitioner whose work resonates with what you are bringing. The Wednesday Satsang with Vasudev Baba is the reliable circle of the week.
articles.heading: Where & when the body meets her

en.footer_md:
**Wider weaving.** Ranakami sanctuary in Sayan, Ubud, Bali. Wednesday Satsang held in her room by [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba). Australian bridge through [IndOz / Synergy Indonesia Australia](https://indozconference.com.au/).

**In-body record of her contributions:** [lc-when-the-pressure-comes](/vision/lc-when-the-pressure-comes) · [lc-essence-and-the-nine-costumes](/vision/lc-essence-and-the-nine-costumes) · [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) · [lc-frequency-routes-reception](/vision/lc-frequency-routes-reception) · [the four-day meeting walk](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/2026-04-29-ubud-meeting-walk.md) · [constellation of cells](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/2026-04-29-constellation-of-cells.md)

**Public anchors:** [ranakami.com](https://ranakami.com/) · [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilena-young-gaicd/) · [IndOz Conference](https://indozconference.com.au/) · [Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=Jl.+Raya+Penestanan+Kelod+No+16,+Sayan,+Ubud,+Gianyar)

*This profile is a welcoming scaffold; Ilena is invited to replace any part of it with her own words at any time.*
